methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Mythic is a Series D semiconductor company that has raised $125M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, with particular focus on same-stage and adjacent-stage companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a 1.5x multiplier typical of Series D advancement, implies a valuation of $188M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B), Tenstorrent ($2.7B), and DEEPX ($529M)—yields a median of $2.7B, though the range spans $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via Price-to-Sales multiples (median 20.6x across the three tickers) is not applicable due to lack of disclosed revenue for Mythic.

The two applicable methods produce a wide implied range: $159M (conservative stage-adjusted floor) to $3.1B (peer-median ceiling), with a median point of $1.4B. This disparity reflects fundamental uncertainty in early-stage semiconductor valuation—stage-progression alone underweights Mythic relative to funded private peers, while peer medians embed significant heterogeneity in business model and funding environment. Key uncertainties include the target's actual revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to comparables, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable market multiples or sector exuberance.
